description: Marqia confirmed she was 'told to come in and help lead' the policy/safety space — directed replacement, not independent decision. Admitted she 'wasn't the person making all the decisions' and the situation is 'really unfair.'

title: Marqia acknowledged delivery risk: 'if there have been issues with delivery in the past and those issues haven't real...
description: Marqia acknowledged delivery risk: 'if there have been issues with delivery in the past and those issues haven't really been resolved amongst the squads then I definitely worry.' Contradicts 'no operational impact' claim.

title: Marqia confirmed team trust at 'zero' — 'I have a team of engineering managers, staff engineers that have zero trust ...
description: Marqia confirmed team trust at 'zero' — 'I have a team of engineering managers, staff engineers that have zero trust right now.'

title: Johan stated 'it's not a performance, it's not a redundancy
description: Johan stated 'it's not a performance, it's not a redundancy. This is something else' and 'it's not a performance, it's not a redundancy. It's more of a future assessment.' Also contradicted prior HRBP Ebba Ljungmark: 'Ebba should not frame it like that, so I don't know where that came from.'

title: Johan stated 'you are one of the best' and 'I know that you've done tremendous job for the space you're in
description: Johan stated 'you are one of the best' and 'I know that you've done tremendous job for the space you're in.' Directly contradicts any performance-based separation rationale.

title: Johan stated 'no separation mutual separation agreement is standard
description: Johan stated 'no separation mutual separation agreement is standard. What is the definition of standard?' Directly undermines his repeated insistence (conversations 4-6) that the agreement is 'standard' and clauses cannot change.

title: Johan stated 'some awkwardness and some insecurity on not knowing what you would do at Spotify
description: Johan stated 'some awkwardness and some insecurity on not knowing what you would do at Spotify.' Third consecutive admission of subjective basis for separation (5th: 'I'm confused'/subjective; 6th: 'stress projection'; 7th: 'awkwardness/insecurity'). Confirms perception problem is emotional, not performance-based.

title: Johan stated 'we initiated the separation termination agreement, and that of course will always be part of the histor...
description: Johan stated 'we initiated the separation termination agreement, and that of course will always be part of the history and the baggage here.' Explicit acknowledgment that Spotify initiated the separation. Prevents future recharacterisation as mutual or employee-driven.

title: Johan stated 'since we agreed on for you to stay on, stock levels, my default solution is to have it unchanged
description: Johan stated 'since we agreed on for you to stay on, stock levels, my default solution is to have it unchanged.' Verbal commitment to maintain salary and equity for IC role.

title: Johan stated 'I was a bit naive saying to you that if you focus on the monetary pieces and come back to me with what ...
description: Johan stated 'I was a bit naive saying to you that if you focus on the monetary pieces and come back to me with what you expect.' Acknowledges his own approach was inappropriate/unrealistic. Weakens future characterisation of Ben as uncooperative on monetary discussion.

title: Adrian's admission: told not to engage with Ben
description: Adrian's admission: told not to engage with Ben. Corroborated by Marqia. Both verbal, confidential, March 2026. Proves redeployment duty was actively obstructed.

title: Adrian departure confirmed: 'I won't be back and that's guaranteed
description: Adrian departure confirmed: 'I won't be back and that's guaranteed.' All Adrian-dependent advocacy (Staff IC, Sarah intro, Johan nudges) has hard expiry ~June 2026.

title: Separation basis shifted four times: Ebba (30 Jan) 'leadership decision'; Johan (5 Feb) 'not performance, not redundancy
description: Separation basis shifted four times: Ebba (30 Jan) 'leadership decision'; Johan (5 Feb) 'not performance, not redundancy... something else' / 'future assessment'; Johan (20 Feb) 'their own stress and anxiety... projected to you'; Johan (3 Mar) 'some awkwardness and some insecurity.' None is a recognised legal ground under LAS S 7.

title: 'Standard agreement' contradiction: Johan spent conversations 4-6 and emails (13 Feb) insisting the agreement is 'sta...
description: 'Standard agreement' contradiction: Johan spent conversations 4-6 and emails (13 Feb) insisting the agreement is 'standard' and cannot change. Wrote 'This is our standard agreement, so we're not in a position to modify it.' Then on 3 Mar stated on tape: 'no separation mutual separation agreement is standard. What is the definition of standard?'

title: 'Mutual' framing vs employer-initiated reality: Ebba framed as 'mutual exploration' (email)
description: 'Mutual' framing vs employer-initiated reality: Ebba framed as 'mutual exploration' (email). Johan admitted 3 Mar: 'we initiated the separation termination agreement.' Non-negotiable terms + employer initiation = not mutual.

title: 'One of the best' + 'Fatal flaw' in same conversation (4th HR): Johan called Ben 'one of the best' and 'tremendous jo...
description: 'One of the best' + 'Fatal flaw' in same conversation (4th HR): Johan called Ben 'one of the best' and 'tremendous job' in the same 79-minute meeting where he labelled ADHD-linked traits a 'limiting fatal flaw.'

title: Adrian confirmed IC offer salary will be 'the same' as current
description: Adrian confirmed IC offer salary will be 'the same' as current. No other formal offer details available yet.

title: Ben saving Spotify from union escalation: Unionen wants to file formal case
description: Ben saving Spotify from union escalation: Unionen wants to file formal case. Ben restraining this. Employer benefits from Ben's cooperation while characterising him as uncooperative.

title: Johan stated 'if you engage in the role, of course, then we need to kind of somehow get over this and turn the page s...
description: Johan stated 'if you engage in the role, of course, then we need to kind of somehow get over this and turn the page somehow.' Implies IC acceptance should reset all history. Creates false binary between fighting for rights and accepting role. Ben's brief 'I agree' needs written correction.

title: Johan stated 'it feels like they are making it harder for you because they've seen these standard agreements hundreds...
description: Johan stated 'it feels like they are making it harder for you because they've seen these standard agreements hundreds of times.' Characterises union as obstructive rather than providing legitimate legal review. Second consecutive offer of Spotify's own representative as faster substitute. Pattern of isolating Ben from independent advice.

title: Ben stated 'I am not from Sweden
description: Ben stated 'I am not from Sweden. This isn't where I'm from. Like this isn't a decision where I'm making a small step...' Reveals outsized personal consequences of leaving Spotify. Could allow attentive HRBP to infer immigration dependency. Cannot be undone.

title: Asymmetric urgency: 14-day HR response gaps while demanding Ben respond 'already next week
description: Asymmetric urgency: 14-day HR response gaps while demanding Ben respond 'already next week.'

title: '14 years / 3 disputes' implicit social pressure — implying only bad-faith employees challenge agreements
description: '14 years / 3 disputes' implicit social pressure — implying only bad-faith employees challenge agreements.

description: Union undermining pattern: characterising Unionen as obstructive, offering employer counsel as substitute.

title: Johan's 'not negotiating' framing: crystallised burden-shift
description: Johan's 'not negotiating' framing: crystallised burden-shift. Employer refused to negotiate, then blamed employee for deadlock. (Seventh HR Conversation, 3 Mar)

title: Temporal pattern of HR non-responsiveness: asymmetric urgency demonstrated across email thread timestamps
description: Temporal pattern of HR non-responsiveness: asymmetric urgency demonstrated across email thread timestamps.

title: Johan made three statements directly following Ben's ADHD disclosure: (1) 'a limiting fatal flaw that will hold you b...
description: Johan made three statements directly following Ben's ADHD disclosure: (1) 'a limiting fatal flaw that will hold you back for this next chapter as a senior engineering manager at Spotify'; (2) 'your, as I call it, fatal flaws that will hold you back or with the baggage from the past'; (3) 'It's not fair towards you to ask you to stretch yourself and grow in those areas because there are limitations there.' No accommodation discussed.

title: Johan's response to ADHD disclosure was 'bring your own coach
description: Johan's response to ADHD disclosure was 'bring your own coach.' No employer accommodation was offered, discussed, or proposed at any point during the meeting. Employer obligation under DL to explore reasonable accommodations was entirely unmet.

title: Johan suggested Ben needs 'coach and mentor' framed around ADHD
description: Johan suggested Ben needs 'coach and mentor' framed around ADHD. Third instance of connecting protected characteristic to separation/transition (after 'fatal flaw' + 'subjective and unresolvable'). PIP-adjacent language.

title: Ben obliquely disclosed shared characteristic (likely neurodivergence) used against him
description: Ben obliquely disclosed shared characteristic (likely neurodivergence) used against him. Marqia's immediate response: 'Don't ever share that we have that thing in common.' She will not serve as corroborating witness — self-preservation instinct dominant.

title: Helena (Unionen) has flagged six specific legal issues with the proposed termination agreement
description: Helena (Unionen) has flagged six specific legal issues with the proposed termination agreement.

Called Ben by wrong name three times (Dan, David, David) in 79-minute meeting. Primary decision-maker (Babar) had met Ben only once.

description: 'Not negotiating' accusation while refusing to negotiate: Johan accused Ben of not being 'concrete' (7th HR) while simultaneously refusing all modifications (email: 'not in a position to modify'). Cannot refuse to negotiate and blame the other party for deadlock.

description: Email thread confirms zero-documentation pattern: all Ben's requests for documentation went unanswered. All documentation that exists weakens Spotify's position.

HIGH RISK — this could block all Content Platform redeployment paths. Counter: Ollie transitioned GPM to IC in same org with zero concern raised.

description: Johan imposing timeline on Staff IC offer with no legal basis — no redundancy declared, no statutory redeployment period applies. Artificial pressure. Adrian coached conditional acceptance framing: 'what happens if it doesn't work out?'

description: Adrian's 'go away then come back' recommendation for ANY transition. His Song DNA analogy: correct advice interpreted as competence-questioning. Ben's counter: he can do the reset, question is whether Marqia/others can move past it. Supports T&S over Content Platform.

description: Adrian's assessment: Sarah Hoyle planning a T&S reorg, suggests reassessing Ben's move 'later this year.'

description: 3 SEM roles and a PPM role exist, only 1 IC role offered: redeployment restricted to single role while broader options available. Artificial scarcity as pressure tactic.

Nico explicitly stated 'I'm not annoyed personally with anybody.' Supports Ben's IC path.

description: All 14 of Ben's direct reports reassigned to Marqia without notification. Ben was still actively managing (expenses, leave approvals) as of the change. No handover, no communication. Classic constructed redundancy pattern (fingerad arbetsbrist). Marqia likely now has 20+ reports cross-timezone — work environment risk under AFS 2015:4.

title: Marqia concerned about 'leaks' and 'filtered' information — suggests she's under pressure to control narrative and in...
description: Marqia concerned about 'leaks' and 'filtered' information — suggests she's under pressure to control narrative and information flow from team.

description: Adrian offering tactical shield: will ask Atelach/Marqia for 'specifics in the moment' re: any concerns about Ben's involvement. Creates documented expectation — if no specifics materialise, preempts future vague allegations.

description: Ben disclosed citizenship application pending and job as anchor to Sweden. Counterparty (Johan/HR) now holds asymmetric leverage on timeline pressure — awareness of immigration dependency creates coercive dynamic.

description: Systematic burden reversal: Ben asked to find his own redeployment, provide counter-offers, name financial numbers, while HR does nothing.
